Details: A pilot scale study using a cast iron-based media found removals from 39 to 73 percent, but the highest removals were achieved using empty bed contact times (EBCTs) much higher than typical in full-scale drinking water applications [2491]. A bench scale study using a micelle–clay complex formed from the interaction of the organic cation ODTMA with montmorillonite achieved 91 percent removal, but also with a relatively high EBCT (greater than 30 minutes) [2492].
